summary: Frontend-only testing for BRILLEXA+ static React marketing site. Verified navbar, routing to all 5 pages (/, /services, /a-propos, /blog, /contact), testimonials with « » guillemets, all 4 services listed, contact hrefs (sms:/mailto:/tel:), shared CTA hrefs, service teaser navigation, and mobile menu toggle. Everything passed. 30/30 UI checks passing.
critical_code_review_comments: SMS_HREF uses 'sms:4385252631?&body=...' — the '?&' is odd (leading ampersand right after '?'). It still works on most devices but the cleaner form is 'sms:4385252631?body=...'. Minor cosmetic.
Testimonials names/quotes are hardcoded fictional. Confirm with the client before publishing to avoid misrepresenting real customers.
External Unsplash image URLs are used directly without lazy-loading (loading='lazy' attribute) which could slow first paint on Blog/Services pages. Optional optimization.
No 404 page — unknown routes silently render Accueil via '*' catch-all. Consider a dedicated NotFound page for UX clarity.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All navbar/routing/contact-href/mobile-menu checks pass on public URL. The only 'FAIL' entries in my initial run for Blog article titles were false negatives caused by reading page.content() before hydration; a follow-up direct check via inner_text confirmed all three articles ('5 habitudes...', 'Un bureau propre...', 'Produits écologiques...') render correctly. No re-test required.
